title: Java Stream Partitioning
description: Partition stream elements into true and false buckets with Collectors.partitioningBy.
learningGoals: Use Collectors.partitioningBy(predicate) to split stream elements into Boolean keys.
Apply a downstream collector such as counting() to summarize each partition.
Avoid confusing partitioningBy with groupingBy over arbitrary classifier keys.

completionChecks.prompt: What keys can appear in the map returned by Collectors.partitioningBy(Task::done)?
choices: Only Boolean keys true and false
A key for every distinct Task instance
No keys until put() is called manually
completionChecks.explanation: partitioningBy always partitions by a Predicate, so the result is keyed by Boolean true and false rather than arbitrary grouping keys.

completionChecks.prompt: What does byDone.get(true) contain after partitioning tasks by Task::done?
choices: All tasks for which done() returned true
A count stored as an Integer
All tasks, because true is the default key
completionChecks.explanation: The single-argument partitioningBy collector stores matching elements in the true list and non-matching elements in the false list.

completionChecks.prompt: What does doneCounts.get(false) return when two unfinished tasks and one finished task are collected?
completionChecks.explanation: With partitioningBy(Task::done, counting()), the false key counts elements where done() is false, so two unfinished tasks produce 2L.
